2605.11182.title: The Many Faces of On-Policy Distillation: Pitfalls, Mechanisms, and Fixes
2605.11182.paper_summary: On-policy distillation (OPD) and on-policy self-distillation (OPSD) have emerged as promising post-training methods for large language models, offering dense token-level supervision on trajectories sampled from the model's own policy. However, existing results on their effectiveness remain mixed: while OP(S)D has shown promise in system prompt and knowledge internalization, recent studies also report instability and degradation. In this work, we present a comprehensive empirical study of when OPD and OPSD work, when they fail, and why. We find that OPD on mathematical reasoning is highly sensitive to teacher choice and loss formulation, whereas OPSD fails in our tested settings due to test-time absence of instance-specific privileged information (PI). In contrast, OPSD is effective when PI represents a shared latent rule, such as a system prompt or alignment preference. We identify three failure mechanisms: (1) distribution mismatch between teacher and student caused by conditioning on student-generated prefixes, (2) optimization instability from biased TopK reverse-KL gradients, and (3) an OPSD-specific limitation where the student learns a PI-free policy that aggregates PI-conditioned teachers, which is insufficient when PI is instance-specific. We further show that stop-gradient TopK objectives, RLVR-adapted teachers, and SFT-stabilized students mitigate these failures.
2605.11182.paper_summary_zh: 在政策蒸餾（OPD）和政策自蒸餾（OPSD）作為大型語言模型的有前景的後訓練方法中，提供了基於從模型自身政策抽樣的軌跡的密集標記級監督。<lb/>然而，現有的效果結果仍然參差不齊：雖然 OP(S)D 在系統提示和知識內化方面顯示出潛力，但最近的研究也報告了不穩定性和退化。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們呈現了一項全面的實證研究，探討 OPD 和 OPSD 何時有效、何時失敗以及原因。<lb/>我們發現，OPD 在數學推理上對教師選擇和損失公式高度敏感，而 OPSD 在我們測試的環境中失效，因為測試時缺乏特定實例的特權信息（PI）。<lb/>相對而言，當 PI 代表共享潛在規則（例如系統提示或對齊偏好）時，OPSD 是有效的。<lb/>我們識別了三種失敗機制：（1）由於依賴學生生成的前綴造成的教師和學生之間的分佈不匹配，（2）來自偏置的 TopK 反向 KL 梯度的優化不穩定性，以及（3）一種特定於 OPSD 的限制，即學生學習了一種無 PI 的政策，聚合了基於 PI 的教師，而當 PI 是特定於實例時這是不夠的。<lb/>我們進一步顯示，停止梯度 TopK 目標、RLVR 調整的教師和 SFT 穩定的學生可以減輕這些失敗。
2605.11182.author: Siqi Zhu et.al.
2605.11182.authors: Siqi Zhu, Xuyan Ye, Hongyu Lu, Weiye Shi, Ge Liu
